---
title: "About Me"
---

I am a geographer and transportation researcher working at the intersection of urban mobility, social equity and changing climate. My work focuses on understanding how everyday travel patterns shape unequal exposure to environmental risks and access to opportunities, and on developing mobility-aware spatial analytics that can inform more equitable urban systems.

Methodologically, I specialize in R-based, reproducible analytical workflows, GIS, and applied spatial analysis. I have experience designing and maintaining research data packages, contributing analytical functions and pipelines, and integrating large‑scale mobility and environmental datasets into transparent, extensible frameworks. Across projects, I aim to translate rigorous spatial methods into tools and evidence that are both scientifically robust and policy‑relevant.

## Academic Positions

-   Currently: Post Doctoral Associate, Western University, 2025-Ongoing. Research funded under the [Western University PostDoctoral Fellowship Program](https://www.uwo.ca/research/funding/students-trainees/postdoctoral-fellows.html).
-   Research Assistant
    -   [Mobilizing Justice](https://mobilizingjustice.ca/), 2021–2025
    -   Transit Research Informed Practice (TRiP) lab, McMaster University, 2019-2021 *Cost-effectiveness, GHG abatement analysis, and stakeholder engagement of EV, alternative fuel, and transit decarbonization policies*
-   Sessional Instructor: [Applied Spatial Statistics](https://paezha.github.io/spatial-analysis-r/), Winter 2023 (check out the Open Education Resources!)
-   Teaching Assistant (various GIS, Spatial Statistics, Transportation, Sustainability, etc.), 2020-2024

## Education

-   PhD, Geography, McMaster University, Completed: 2025
-   MASc, Transportation Engineering, McMaster University, Completed: 2021
-   BEng, Civil Engineering & Society (Min. Geographic Information Systems) with a placement in the private and public sector, McMaster University, Completed: 2020

## Research Presentations

Browse the interactive map of my research presentations, seminars, workshops, and lightning talks (Updated Sept 2025):

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
library(leaflet)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)

# Read CSV
talks <- read_csv("data/talks.csv") |> 
  filter(!is.na(Lat) & !is.na(Long))

# Define icons for each 'What' type
icons <- awesomeIcons(
  icon = case_when(
    talks$What == "Presentation" ~ "star",
    talks$What == "Workshop" ~ "cog",
    talks$What == "Seminar" ~ "graduation-cap",
    talks$What == "Lightning presentation" ~ "bolt",
    TRUE ~ "circle"
  ),
  iconColor = "white",
  markerColor = case_when(
    talks$What == "Presentation" ~ "blue",
    talks$What == "Workshop" ~ "red",
    talks$What == "Seminar" ~ "green",
    talks$What == "Lightning presentation" ~ "purple",
    TRUE ~ "gray"
  ),
  library = "fa"
)

# Create leaflet map with clustering and legend
leaflet(talks) %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addAwesomeMarkers(
    ~Long, ~Lat,
    icon = icons,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<strong>Title:</strong> ", R_Title, "<br>",
      "<strong>Year:</strong> ", Years, "<br>",
      "<strong>Organization:</strong> ", Organization, "<br>",
      "<strong>Where:</strong> ", Where, "<br>",
      "<strong>Role:</strong> ", Role, "<br>",
      "<strong>Type:</strong> ", What
    ),
    clusterOptions = markerClusterOptions()
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    position = "topright",
    colors = c("dodgerblue", "#CD0000", "forestgreen", "#DA70D6"),
    labels = c(
      "<i class='fa fa-star'></i> Presentation",
      "<i class='fa fa-cog'></i> Workshop",
      "<i class='fa fa-graduation-cap'></i> Seminar",
      "<i class='fa fa-bolt'></i> Lightning talk"
    ),
    opacity = 1,
    title = NULL,  # remove legend title
    labFormat = labelFormat(html = TRUE)
  )


```

## Interested in more details?

-   GitHub: [soukhova](https://github.com/soukhova)
-   ORCID: [0000-0003-4371-4831](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-4371-4831)
-   [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/anastasia-soukhov-b4ab09111/)

Download the full CV [\[PDF\]](data/cv.pdf) (Updated Sept 2025)
